title: Phalangium angustifolium
intro: Phalangium angustifolium, also known as the Narrow-leaved Mallow, is a perennial plant species in the family Malvaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which have been traditionally used to treat a variety of ailments, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Phalangium angustifolium include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is commonly used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory conditions.
taxonomy: Phalangium angustifolium is a species of harvestman, which is a type of arachnid that belongs to the class Arachnida. It is a member of the infraorder Opiliones and the family Phalangiidae. Harvestmen are often classified as arachnids due to their body structure and the presence of eight legs. Phalangium angustifolium's classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history.
